days.title: The Cloud That Won't Quite Break
days.genre: warm afro-house-lite / neo-soul — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying a Bb mixolydian I–bVII–IV–vi comp over a steady four-on-the-floor RolandTR909 kit with an offbeat clap and a shaker, a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline in the gaps, sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's bright threads, a plucked acoustic bass walking the roots under a soft sine sub; mixolydian's lowered seventh (natural Ab over the Bb root) keeping the warmth honest and just-off-level — record markets and good science lifting one column while the war holds the other down, neither rising nor sinking

days.title: A Hair Off the Floor
days.genre: flute-led cinematic soul / slow downtempo — a sampled gm_flute tracing a circling Eb melodic-minor topline over warm gm_epiano1 electric-piano chords, a bowed-string pad holding the weight, a plucked gm_acoustic_bass walking the roots with a sine sub beneath, a soft brushed RhythmAce kit through light reverb, and sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's two bright threads; melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — a heavy war-morning held a hair off the floor as the market's dread eased, and deliberately no harp, piano or clarinet after the last week's run

days.title: A Toll on Every Hull
days.genre: harp-led dark chamber post-rock / drone — a sampled gm_orchestral_harp plucking a circling Ab phrygian topline over a slow bowed-string drone, a plucked gm_acoustic_bass walking the low roots with a sine sub beneath, a muffled RolandTR909 heartbeat kick and a distant AkaiLinn cross-stick through deep tape-echo, and sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's few bright threads; phrygian's flat second (A natural leaning against the Ab root) tilting the whole thing toward dread without collapse — a blockade-morning day held as weight rather than dramatized, and deliberately no piano or clarinet after yesterday's dirge

days.title: Not Possible at Present
days.genre: cinematic dirge / dark chamber post-rock — a low, mournful gm_clarinet carrying the lead over a slow bowed-string drone in C# aeolian, a sampled grand piano tolling deep single octaves like a funeral bell, a muffled RolandTR808 heartbeat kick with a distant EmuSP12 cross-stick, a sine sub holding the floor, and sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's few bright threads; aeolian's plain natural minor sitting well below the line — an open-war day that broke a barely-trusted pause, held as grief rather than dramatized

days.title: The Lull and the Locked Missiles
days.genre: dusty trip-hop / neo-soul — a sampled vibraphone laying warm F# mixolydian chords over a laid-back RhythmAce kit with tape-echo, a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline in the gaps, sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's quiet wins, a warm sawtooth bass walking under a sine sub; mixolydian's lowered-seventh natural E keeping the warmth honest and just-off-level — a mixed day where real danger and real progress land even, neither rising nor sinking

days.title: The Quiet Was Only the Guns
days.genre: late-night piano-trio nocturne / slow noir-soul — a sampled grand piano comping loose B aeolian voicings over a brushed EmuSP12 kit and a plucked pizzicato upright bass walking the low end, a sampled clarinet tracing a smoky, circling topline in the gaps, a faint bowed-string pad and a soft sine sub holding the warmth, with sparse glockenspiel glints for the two quiet wins; aeolian's plain natural minor sitting a shade below level — a day of heavy weather and worsening outbreaks held, not dramatized, over a market that stayed calm and a war that went quiet for a night

days.title: Second Day, Steady Hands
days.genre: brass-led dark electronic / cinematic drum-and-bass — a sampled muted trumpet and low brass tracing a circling E harmonic minor topline over a broken, dub-spaced RolandTR909 pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled vibraphone pricking mallet accents in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub, and a low bowed-string pad holding the dread; harmonic minor's raised seventh (D#) dragging hard against its lowered sixth (C) — the exact half-step tension of a day that trades fire for a second day yet lets mediators and a rising market pull it back a notch, neither breaking nor resolving

days.title: The Word Was Over
days.genre: industrial-tinged dark-electronic / chamber-strings noir — a bowed cello-and-viola string ensemble tracing a circling A phrygian topline over a driving, dub-spaced LinnDrum pulse with deep tape-echo, sampled pizzicato strings pricking the offbeats, a clean tremolo electric-guitar answering low in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a deep sine sub, and a bowed-string drone holding the dread; phrygian's flat second (Bb over A) leaning the whole thing toward a truce that broke without letting it collapse — oil climbing, sirens back on, and two quiet wins refusing to be drowned out

days.title: Strait Below Neutral
days.genre: motorik / dub-techno — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous D melodic minor chords over a steady, driving TR-909 four-on-the-floor pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling topline in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub and a low bowed-string pad holding the unease; melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — forward motion under a heavy sky, a day that tilts a shade down but refuses to fall

days.title: Two Ledgers Open at Once
days.genre: gospel-soul / afrobeat-tinged organ groove — a sampled Hammond-style organ laying warm G mixolydian gospel chords over a steady swung four-on-the-floor kit with afrobeat shaker and rim work, muted brass stabs answering in the gaps, a plucked electric bass walking under a soft sine sub, and high glockenspiel glints for the quiet wins; mixolydian's lowered seventh (natural F) keeping the warmth honest and just-off-level — a day that tilts a shade toward strain but refuses to fall, two ledgers open at once

days.title: Eyewall Over Rota
days.genre: liquid drum-and-bass / dark-electronic — a fast, broken AkaiLinn+909 pulse with tape-echo driving under a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline, a Rhodes-ish electric-piano laying ambiguous dorian chords, a plucked upright bass walking with a deep sine sub, and a low bowed-string pad holding the unease; C dorian's raised sixth keeping the minor from sinking — fast feet under a heavy sky, storm on one side and a stadium roar on the other

days.title: The Grand Mosalla at Noon
days.genre: noir spy-jazz / cinematic-mystery — a brushed AkaiLinn kit walking soft under a plucked upright bass and a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling F harmonic minor topline, a muted clean electric-guitar line answering in the gaps, a low bowed-string pad holding the dread and a warm sine sub far below, all through deep tape-echo; harmonic minor's raised seventh dragging hard against its lowered sixth — the exact half-step tension of a day that will not resolve either way

days.title: Half-Staff on the Fourth
days.genre: dub-tinged post-rock / cinematic — sampled grand piano laying slow open aeolian chords under a swelling bed of bowed strings, a brushed RhythmAce kit marching soft and spacious through deep tape-echo, a plucked upright bass and a low sine sub holding the floor, with sparse glockenspiel glints for the quiet wins; Bb aeolian's plain natural minor sitting heavy and steady, grief held rather than dramatized on a hot, flag-lowered Fourth

days.title: A Day of Mourning, and the Wins Come Quiet
days.genre: post-punk / darkwave — a sampled clean-and-tremolo electric guitar tracing a circling phrygian topline over a driving, dub-spaced TR-808 pulse with tape-echo, a low bowed-string pad holding the dread and a plucked upright bass walking under a deep sine sub, with sparse glockenspiel glints for the quiet wins; Eb phrygian's flat second leaning the whole thing toward grief without collapsing — flags at half-staff, but the science still lands

days.title: The Ledger Tilts
days.genre: warm downtempo / clavinet-and-vibraphone neo-soul — a sampled Wurlitzer-style electric piano and a sampled clavinet trading a loose, swung comp over a laid-back RhythmAce kit with tape-echo, a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling topline in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub and a low bowed-string pad; Ab mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the warmth honest and just-off-level — a day that tilts a shade toward strain but refuses to fall

days.title: Born On This Soil
days.genre: dub-techno / motorik house-lite — a sampled xylophone tracing a circling topline over a steady, driving TR-707 four-on-the-floor pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled celesta answering high in the gaps, a Rhodes-ish electric-piano pad laying ambiguous chords, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub; C# melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — a landmark held up against a heavy ledger, neither rising nor sinking

days.title: Records Above a Held Breath
days.genre: neo-soul / dub-tinged groove — a clean sampled electric guitar tracing a circling topline and a glockenspiel answering high in the gaps, over a steady dub-spaced TR-808 kit with deep tape-echo, a plucked upright bass walking under sampled-piano comp chords and a soft sine sub; F# mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and unsettled — a market running hot above a held breath, never quite triumphant, never quite sinking

days.title: Front-Running the Calm
days.genre: marimba-led trip-hop / neo-soul — a sampled marimba tracing a circling topline over a slow, swung AkaiLinn kit with deep tape-echo, a sampled harp answering in the gaps, a plucked upright bass and a soft sine sub holding the floor under a low bowed-string pad; B melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor from sinking, a market staying oddly calm above a fragile reality

days.title: Sixteen Hundred Strangers
days.genre: motorik / krautrock — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying patient dorian chords over a steady, driving four-on-the-floor pulse, with a sampled vibraphone and a high flute tracing a circling topline through tape-echo, a plucked upright bass walking underneath and a sine sub holding the floor; E dorian's raised sixth keeping the minor from collapsing into grief — forward motion under a heavy sky, like a relief column that just keeps moving

days.title: Fifty Thousand Names
days.genre: slow dub-ballad / spiritual-downtempo — a sampled flute tracing a low, circling topline over a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano and a dusty, dub-spaced EmuSP12 kit with deep tape-echo, a plucked upright bass, a sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath; A aeolian's plain natural minor sitting heavy and patient, grief held steady rather than dramatized, with one warm thread of light running through it

days.title: Doublet
days.genre: darkwave drum-and-bass — a TR-909-led broken pulse driving under a tremolo electric-guitar topline that circles a lowered-second figure, with a deep sine sub-bass, low bowed-string drone and tape-echo guitar stabs; D phrygian's flat second leaning the whole thing toward dread without collapsing — fast feet under a heavy heart

days.title: Hottest Day Since '47
days.genre: post-rock / cinematic-strings — sampled grand piano laying slow open chords under a single sustained electric-guitar topline that swells and decays, with a bed of bowed strings, a marching brushed RhythmAce kit and a sine sub far below; G aeolian's natural minor sitting heavy but never sinking, a heat-haze that holds rather than breaks

days.title: Forty-Three in the Shade
days.genre: industrial-tinged downtempo / dub — a sampled clarinet tracing a dark, circling topline over a dub-spaced, slightly metallic RhythmAce kit with deep tape-echo, a processed muted-guitar pulse, a sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath; C harmonic minor's raised seventh dragging hard against the lowered sixth, an uneasy tension under a surface that refuses to break either way

days.title: Two Directions at Once
days.genre: vibraphone-led darkwave / dub-trip-hop — a sampled vibraphone tracing a circling minor topline over a slow, dub-spaced AkaiLinn kit with deep tape-echo, a soft sine sub and a low bowed-string darkwave pad underneath, with sparse muted-guitar glints; F melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh lifting the minor third just enough to keep the calm honest and unsettled — neither rising nor sinking

days.title: Encouraging, They Said
days.genre: chamber-pop / acoustic small-ensemble — pizzicato string ensemble and a sampled marimba trading a circling figure over a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, a plucked upright bass and a low clarinet line, with a warm bowed-string pad and high oboe glints underneath; Bb mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and unsettled, neither rising nor sinking

days.title: Closed Again, Still Flowing
days.genre: downtempo neo-soul / dusk-electronic — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous chords over a slow, dusty AkaiLinn kit with a soft clap, a deep sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath, a sampled vibraphone tracing a sparse, hopeful topline with tape-echo; Eb melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor third unresolved — uneasy, but refusing to go dark

days.title: Switzerland Stayed Empty
days.genre: motorik / dub-techno — a sampled kalimba and vibraphone tracing a circular topline over a steady, driving TR-909 pulse with tape-echo, a deep sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath; Ab dorian's raised sixth keeping the minor key from settling into gloom, neither rising nor giving in

days.title: Off Paper, Onto Water
days.genre: neo-soul / dub-tinged — sampled marimba leading a warm topline over a spacious TR-808 with tape-echo, soft muted-brass stabs, a plucked upright bass and a sine sub, with a bowed-string pad underneath; C# mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and not quite settled

days.title: Signed Not Sealed
days.genre: downtempo trip-hop / neo-soul — a muted Rhodes-ish electric piano laying ambiguous chords over a slow brushed RhythmAce kit, a soft sub-bass and a bowed-string drone, with sparse marimba glints; F# melodic minor's raised sixth and seventh keeping the minor third unresolved, neither settling nor darkening — a held breath

days.title: A Page and a Half
days.genre: ambient folk-pop / chamber — fingerpicked nylon guitar leading over warm bowed strings and a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, with a plucked upright bass and high flute glints; B mixolydian's lowered seventh keeping the brightness honest and unfinished

days.title: Two Days From Friday
days.genre: gospel-soul / chamber-pop — sampled grand piano leading warm gospel-voiced chords over a soft brushed RhythmAce kit, a plucked upright bass and high mallet sparkle, E ionian opening bright but holding one hand back

days.title: Switzerland by Friday
days.genre: bright indie-pop / afrobeat-tinged — sampled vibraphone riff and mallets over a crisp TR-909 lite kit, plucked guitar topline and a warm sine sub, A lydian's raised fourth opening the room without losing its guard

days.title: Open To All, Tomorrow
days.genre: dub / neo-soul — sampled electric-piano chords over a spacious TR-808 with tape-echo delays, plucked guitar topline

days.title: Final, Agreed-Upon Text
days.genre: motorik / krautrock, Rhodes-ish electric piano over a TR-909 pulse

days.title: Off the Ledge
days.genre: trip-hop / neo-soul, vibraphone-led

days.genre: downtempo, dorian

days.genre: slow piano ballad

days.genre: slow piano ballad, dorian
